sentences.sentence: For millennia, the coconut has been central to the lives of Polynesian and Asian peoples.
sentences.translation: 数千年来，椰子一直是波利尼西亚和亚洲人民生活中的核心组成部分。

structure.text: to the lives of Polynesian and Asian peoples

difficult_points: For millennia 表示极长时间范围，和现在完成时 has been 呼应，强调这种重要性一直延续。
central to 表示“对……至关重要”，不是空间意义上的“在中心”。
Polynesian and Asian peoples 中 peoples 指不同族群或民族，而不是泛指“人们”。

sentences.sentence: In the western world, on the other hand, coconuts have always been exotic and unusual, sometimes rare.
sentences.translation: 而在西方世界，椰子却一直显得充满异域色彩、不同寻常，有时甚至十分罕见。

structure.text: have always been exotic and unusual

difficult_points: on the other hand 与上一句形成对比，说明椰子在西方世界的地位不同。
have always been 用现在完成时强调从过去到现在椰子在西方一直被视为异域之物。
sometimes rare 是补充说明，进一步强化 exotic and unusual 的程度。

sentences.sentence: The Italian merchant traveller Marco Polo apparently saw coconuts in South Asia in the late 13th century, and among the mid-14th-century travel writings of Sir John Mandeville there is mention of 'great Notes of Ynde' (great Nuts of India).
sentences.translation: 意大利商人兼旅行家马可·波罗显然在13世纪末于南亚见过椰子，而在约翰·曼德维尔爵士14世纪中期的旅行著作中，也提到了“great Notes of Ynde”（印度的大坚果）。

structure.text: The Italian merchant traveller Marco Polo
in South Asia in the late 13th century
and among the mid-14th-century travel writings of Sir John Mandeville there is mention of 'great Notes of Ynde' (great Nuts of India)
among the mid-14th-century travel writings of Sir John Mandeville
there is mention of 'great Notes of Ynde' (great Nuts of India)

difficult_points: merchant traveller 是 Marco Polo 的身份说明，整体作主语的一部分。
apparently 表示作者依据记录作出的推断，不等于绝对确定。
and 后连接的是另一个完整信息单元，其中 there is mention of 表示“有提及”。
括号中的 great Nuts of India 是对古写法 'great Notes of Ynde' 的解释。

sentences.sentence: Today, images of palm-fringed tropical beaches are clichés in the west to sell holidays, chocolate bars, fizzy drinks and even romance.
sentences.translation: 如今，在西方，用棕榈环绕的热带海滩图像来推销度假、巧克力棒、汽水，甚至浪漫，已经成了陈词滥调。

structure.text: to sell holidays, chocolate bars, fizzy drinks and even romance

difficult_points: images 是主语核心，of palm-fringed tropical beaches 后置修饰这些图像。
clichés 指被反复使用而显得俗套的表达或图像。
to sell... 表目的，说明这些热带海滩图像被商业化使用。

sentences.sentence: Typically, we envisage coconuts as brown cannonballs that, when opened, provide sweet white flesh.
sentences.translation: 通常，我们会把椰子想象成棕色的炮弹；它们一旦被打开，就会露出甜甜的白色果肉。

structure.text: that, when opened, provide sweet white flesh

difficult_points: envisage ... as ... 表示“把……想象成……”，后面 brown cannonballs 是比喻。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 brown cannonballs，也就是人们眼中的椰子。
when opened 是省略结构，完整含义是 when they are opened。

sentences.sentence: But we see only part of the fruit and none of the plant from which they come.
sentences.translation: 但我们看到的只是这种果实的一部分，却完全看不到结出它们的植物本身。

structure.text: see only part of the fruit and none of the plant

difficult_points: only part of the fruit 与 none of the plant 构成对照，说明日常印象很片面。
from which they come 是介词前置的定语从句，which 指 the plant。
they 指前文的 coconuts，而不是 people 或其他复数名词。

sentences.sentence: The coconut palm has a smooth, slender, grey trunk, up to 30 metres tall.
sentences.translation: 椰子树有一根光滑、细长的灰色树干，高度可达30米。
difficult_points: The coconut palm 指整棵椰子树，不是单个椰子果实。
a smooth, slender, grey trunk 中多个形容词连续修饰 trunk。
up to 30 metres tall 是后置补充说明，表示最高可达到的高度。

sentences.sentence: This is an important source of timber for building houses, and is increasingly being used as a replacement for endangered hardwoods in the furniture construction industry.
sentences.translation: 它是建造房屋的重要木材来源，并且越来越多地被家具制造业用作濒危硬木的替代品。

structure.text: is an important source of timber for building houses
and is increasingly being used as a replacement for endangered hardwoods in the furniture construction industry
as a replacement for endangered hardwoods

difficult_points: This 指上一句中的树干，而不是整段话或椰子果实。
is increasingly being used 是现在进行时被动语态，强调用途正在增加。
as a replacement for... 表示“作为……的替代品”，说明椰木被用于取代濒危硬木。

sentences.sentence: The trunk is surmounted by a rosette of leaves, each of which may be up to six metres long.
sentences.translation: 树干顶端长着一簇呈莲座状排列的叶子，每片叶子都可能长达六米。

structure.text: is surmounted by a rosette of leaves
each of which may be up to six metres long

difficult_points: is surmounted by 是被动结构，表示树干顶端被叶簇覆盖或顶着叶簇。
a rosette of leaves 指叶片呈放射状或莲座状聚集。
each of which 引导非限制性定语从句，which 指 leaves。

sentences.sentence: The leaves have hard veins in their centres which, in many parts of the world, are used as brushes after the green part of the leaf has been stripped away.
sentences.translation: 叶子中央有坚硬的叶脉；在世界许多地方，绿色叶片部分被剥去之后，这些叶脉会被用作刷子。

structure.text: have hard veins in their centres
which, in many parts of the world, are used as brushes after the green part of the leaf has been stripped away
in many parts of the world
after the green part of the leaf has been stripped away

difficult_points: which 引导定语从句，指 hard veins，而不是 centres。
are used as brushes 是被动结构，说明叶脉被当作刷子使用。
after 引导时间状语从句，其中 has been stripped away 是现在完成时被动语态。

expressions.text: in many parts of the world

sentences.sentence: Immature coconut flowers are tightly clustered together among the leaves at the top of the trunk.
sentences.translation: 未成熟的椰子花紧密地聚集在树干顶部的叶片之间。

structure.text: at the top of the trunk

difficult_points: Immature 修饰 coconut flowers，表示花尚未成熟。
are tightly clustered together 是被动含义的状态描述，强调花紧密成簇。
among the leaves at the top of the trunk 说明花的位置在树冠顶部叶片之间。

sentences.sentence: The flower stems may be tapped for their sap to produce a drink, and the sap can also be reduced by boiling to produce a type of sugar used for cooking.
sentences.translation: 花茎可以被割取汁液来制作饮料，而这种汁液也可以通过煮沸浓缩，制成一种用于烹饪的糖。

structure.text: may be tapped for their sap
and the sap can also be reduced by boiling
to produce a type of sugar used for cooking

difficult_points: may be tapped 是情态动词加被动语态，表示花茎可被切开或割取汁液。
for their sap 表目的或对象，说明割取的是汁液。
can also be reduced by boiling 中 reduce 指通过煮沸使液体浓缩。
used for cooking 后置修饰 a type of sugar。

sentences.sentence: Coconut palms produce as many as seventy fruits per year, weighing more than a kilogram each.
sentences.translation: 椰子树每年最多能结出70个果实，每个重量超过一公斤。

structure.text: produce as many as seventy fruits per year
weighing more than a kilogram each

difficult_points: as many as 强调数量之多，表示“多达”。
per year 表示按每年计算。
weighing more than a kilogram each 是现在分词短语，补充说明这些果实各自的重量。

sentences.sentence: The wall of the fruit has three layers: a waterproof outer layer, a fibrous middle layer and a hard, inner layer.
sentences.translation: 椰子果实的果壁有三层：防水的外层、纤维质的中层和坚硬的内层。

structure.text: a waterproof outer layer, a fibrous middle layer and a hard, inner layer

difficult_points: 冒号后面的三个名词短语具体列举 three layers 的内容。
outer、middle、inner 按从外到内的顺序说明果壁结构。
a hard, inner layer 中 hard 和 inner 共同修饰 layer。

sentences.sentence: The thick fibrous middle layer produces coconut fibre, 'coir', which has numerous uses and is particularly important in manufacturing ropes.
sentences.translation: 厚厚的纤维质中层会产生椰子纤维，即“椰壳纤维”；这种纤维用途众多，在制造绳索方面尤其重要。

structure.text: which has numerous uses and is particularly important in manufacturing ropes
has numerous uses and is particularly important

difficult_points: 'coir' 是 coconut fibre 的同位语，给出这种纤维的专名。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 coconut fibre。
is particularly important in manufacturing ropes 说明椰壳纤维在制绳中的突出用途。

sentences.sentence: The woody innermost layer, the shell, with its three prominent 'eyes', surrounds the seed.
sentences.translation: 最里面的木质层，也就是椰壳，带有三个明显的“眼”，包围着种子。
difficult_points: the shell 是 The woody innermost layer 的同位语，说明最内层就是椰壳。
with its three prominent 'eyes' 是插入的补充信息，不是主谓结构。
surrounds 的主语仍是 The woody innermost layer，而不是 eyes。

sentences.sentence: An important product obtained from the shell is charcoal, which is widely used in various industries as well as in the home as a cooking fuel.
sentences.translation: 从椰壳中获得的一种重要产品是木炭；木炭广泛用于各种工业，也在家庭中作为烹饪燃料使用。

structure.text: An important product obtained from the shell
which is widely used in various industries as well as in the home as a cooking fuel
in various industries as well as in the home

difficult_points: obtained from the shell 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 product。
which 指 charcoal，而不是 shell。
as well as 连接两个使用场景：various industries 和 the home。
as a cooking fuel 表示木炭在家庭中的用途。

sentences.sentence: When broken in half, the shells are also used as bowls in many parts of Asia.
sentences.translation: 椰壳被劈成两半后，在亚洲许多地方也会被用作碗。
difficult_points: When broken in half 是省略结构，完整含义是 when the shells are broken in half。
are also used as bowls 是被动结构，说明椰壳的另一种用途。
in many parts of Asia 限定这种用途出现的地区。

sentences.sentence: Inside the shell are the nutrients (endosperm) needed by the developing seed.
sentences.translation: 椰壳内部有发育中的种子所需要的营养物质，也就是胚乳。

structure.text: the nutrients (endosperm) needed by the developing seed

difficult_points: 句首 Inside the shell 触发倒装，真正的主语是 the nutrients (endosperm)。
endosperm 是对 nutrients 的括号解释，表示胚乳。
needed by the developing seed 是过去分词短语，说明这些营养物质被发育中的种子所需要。

sentences.sentence: Initially, the endosperm is a sweetish liquid, coconut water, which is enjoyed as a drink, but also provides the hormones which encourage other plants to grow more rapidly and produce higher yields.
sentences.translation: 最初，胚乳是一种略带甜味的液体，也就是椰子水；它既可以作为饮料享用，也能提供促进其他植物更快生长并提高产量的激素。

structure.text: which is enjoyed as a drink, but also provides the hormones which encourage other plants to grow more rapidly and produce higher yields
which encourage other plants to grow more rapidly and produce higher yields

difficult_points: coconut water 是 a sweetish liquid 的同位语，说明这种液体就是椰子水。
第一个 which 指 coconut water，后面并列说明它可饮用并能提供激素。
第二个 which 指 hormones，这些激素促进其他植物生长和增产。
to grow more rapidly and produce higher yields 是并列不定式结构，共同补足 encourage 的结果。

sentences.sentence: As the fruit matures, the coconut water gradually solidifies to form the brilliant white, fat-rich, edible flesh or meat.
sentences.translation: 随着果实成熟，椰子水逐渐凝固，形成亮白色、富含脂肪、可食用的果肉。

structure.text: to form the brilliant white, fat-rich, edible flesh or meat

difficult_points: As 引导时间状语从句，表示“随着果实成熟”。
solidifies 表示从液体逐渐变成固体。
brilliant white、fat-rich、edible 三个形容词共同修饰 flesh or meat。

sentences.sentence: Dried coconut flesh, 'copra', is made into coconut oil and coconut milk, which are widely used in cooking in different parts of the world, as well as in cosmetics.
sentences.translation: 干椰肉，即“椰干”，会被制成椰子油和椰奶；二者在世界不同地区广泛用于烹饪，也用于化妆品。

structure.text: is made into coconut oil and coconut milk
which are widely used in cooking in different parts of the world, as well as in cosmetics
in cooking in different parts of the world

difficult_points: 'copra' 是 Dried coconut flesh 的同位语，给出干椰肉的名称。
is made into 是被动语态，表示原料被加工成 coconut oil and coconut milk。
which 指 coconut oil and coconut milk，而不是 copra。
as well as in cosmetics 补充说明除烹饪外的另一种应用领域。

sentences.sentence: A derivative of coconut fat, glycerine, acquired strategic importance in a quite different sphere, as Alfred Nobel introduced the world to his nitroglycerine-based invention: dynamite.
sentences.translation: 椰子脂肪的一种衍生物——甘油——在一个截然不同的领域获得了战略重要性，因为阿尔弗雷德·诺贝尔向世界推出了他以硝化甘油为基础的发明：炸药。

structure.text: as Alfred Nobel introduced the world to his nitroglycerine-based invention: dynamite

difficult_points: glycerine 是 A derivative of coconut fat 的同位语，说明这种衍生物就是甘油。
acquired strategic importance 表示获得战略意义，暗示它与炸药等重要技术相关。
as 引导原因或时间背景从句，解释甘油为何在不同领域变得重要。
冒号后的 dynamite 具体说明 Nobel 的发明。

sentences.sentence: Their biology would appear to make coconuts the great maritime voyagers and coastal colonizers of the plant world.
sentences.translation: 椰子的生物学特性似乎使它们成为植物界伟大的海上旅行者和海岸殖民者。

structure.text: the great maritime voyagers and coastal colonizers of the plant world

difficult_points: Their 指椰子的，biology 指其生物学特征，而不是生物学学科本身。
would appear to make 表示作者较谨慎的判断，意为“似乎使……成为”。
maritime voyagers and coastal colonizers 是拟人化表达，形容椰子善于海上漂流并在海岸定居。

sentences.sentence: The large, energy-rich fruits are able to float in water and tolerate salt, but cannot remain viable indefinitely; studies suggest after about 110 days at sea they are no longer able to germinate.
sentences.translation: 这些大而富含能量的果实能够漂浮在水中并耐受盐分，但不能无限期地保持生命力；研究表明，它们在海上漂流约110天后便不再能够发芽。

structure.text: are able to float in water and tolerate salt
after about 110 days at sea they are no longer able to germinate

difficult_points: are able to 后并列 float 和 tolerate，说明椰子适合海上漂流的两个特性。
remain viable 表示保持能发芽或存活的能力，不只是“可行”。
分号后 studies suggest 引出研究结论，after about 110 days at sea 是时间条件。
no longer able to germinate 表示已失去发芽能力。

expressions.meaning_zh: 保持生命力；保持可发芽性

sentences.sentence: Literally cast onto desert island shores, with little more than sand to grow in and exposed to the full glare of the tropical sun, coconut seeds are able to germinate and root.
sentences.translation: 椰子种子被真正抛上海岛荒滩，即便几乎只有沙地可供生长，并且暴露在热带烈日的强光下，也能够发芽并扎根。

structure.text: Literally cast onto desert island shores
with little more than sand to grow in
and exposed to the full glare of the tropical sun
are able to germinate and root

difficult_points: 句首多个修饰成分描述椰子种子被冲上海岸后的严酷环境。
with little more than sand to grow in 表示几乎只有沙子作为生长介质。
exposed to... 是过去分词短语，说明种子暴露在强烈阳光下。
germinate and root 是并列动词，表示发芽并扎根。

sentences.sentence: The air pocket in the seed, created as the endosperm solidifies, protects the embryo.
sentences.translation: 种子内部的气囊是在胚乳凝固时形成的，它保护着胚胎。

structure.text: The air pocket in the seed

difficult_points: created as the endosperm solidifies 是插入的过去分词短语，说明 air pocket 的形成时间。
as 在这里表示“随着；当……时”，不是“因为”的主要含义。
protects 的主语是 The air pocket，而不是 endosperm。

sentences.sentence: In addition, the fibrous fruit wall that helped it to float during the voyage stores moisture that can be taken up by the roots of the coconut seedling as it starts to grow.
sentences.translation: 此外，帮助椰子在漂流过程中浮起的纤维质果壁还能储存水分；当椰子幼苗开始生长时，这些水分可由其根部吸收。

structure.text: the fibrous fruit wall that helped it to float during the voyage
that helped it to float during the voyage
that can be taken up by the roots of the coconut seedling as it starts to grow

difficult_points: 第一个 that 修饰 the fibrous fruit wall，说明果壁曾帮助椰子漂浮。
it 在 helped it to float 中指椰子或果实本身。
第二个 that 修饰 moisture，说明水分可以被幼苗根部吸收。
as it starts to grow 中 it 指 the coconut seedling。

sentences.sentence: There have been centuries of academic debate over the origins of the coconut.
sentences.translation: 关于椰子的起源，学界已经争论了数百年。

structure.text: There have been centuries of academic debate
over the origins of the coconut

difficult_points: There have been 是存在句的现在完成时，强调争论持续了很长时间。
centuries of academic debate 表示“数百年的学术争论”，不是具体几个世纪的课程。
over 在这里表示“关于、围绕”，引出争论主题。

sentences.sentence: There were no coconut palms in West Africa, the Caribbean or the east coast of the Americas before the voyages of the European explorers Vasco da Gama and Columbus in the late 15th and early 16th centuries.
sentences.translation: 在欧洲探险家瓦斯科·达·伽马和哥伦布于15世纪末至16世纪初的航行之前，西非、加勒比地区以及美洲东海岸都没有椰子树。

structure.text: in West Africa, the Caribbean or the east coast of the Americas
before the voyages of the European explorers Vasco da Gama and Columbus
in the late 15th and early 16th centuries

difficult_points: There were no... 是存在句否定形式，说明这些地区当时不存在椰子树。
West Africa, the Caribbean or the east coast of the Americas 是并列地点范围。
before the voyages... 给出时间界限，限定“没有椰子树”的历史阶段。
in the late 15th and early 16th centuries 修饰探险航行发生的时期。

expressions.text: in the late 15th and early 16th centuries

sentences.sentence: 16th century trade and human migration patterns reveal that Arab traders and European sailors are likely to have moved coconuts from South and Southeast Asia to Africa and then across the Atlantic to the east coast of America.
sentences.translation: 16世纪的贸易和人类迁徙模式表明，阿拉伯商人和欧洲水手很可能把椰子从南亚和东南亚带到非洲，然后又越过大西洋带到美洲东海岸。

structure.text: 16th century trade and human migration patterns
that Arab traders and European sailors are likely to have moved coconuts from South and Southeast Asia to Africa and then across the Atlantic to the east coast of America
are likely to have moved coconuts
from South and Southeast Asia to Africa and then across the Atlantic to the east coast of America

difficult_points: reveal 后接 that 引导的宾语从句，说明贸易和迁徙模式揭示的内容。
are likely to have moved 表示对过去行为的推测，含义是“很可能已经移动或传播”。
from... to... and then across... to... 描述椰子传播路线的连续阶段。
South and Southeast Asia 指南亚和东南亚两个相邻区域。

sentences.sentence: But the origin of coconuts discovered along the west coast of America by 16th century sailors has been the subject of centuries of discussion.
sentences.translation: 但是，16世纪水手在美洲西海岸发现的椰子的起源，已经成为数百年来讨论的主题。

structure.text: the origin of coconuts discovered along the west coast of America by 16th century sailors
discovered along the west coast of America by 16th century sailors
has been the subject of centuries of discussion

difficult_points: But 表明接下来讨论的问题与前面较清楚的传播路线不同。
discovered... 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 coconuts。
has been the subject of... 用现在完成时强调这一争论持续至今或持续很久。
the origin 是句子主语核心，不要误把 coconuts 当主语核心。

sentences.sentence: Two diametrically opposed origins have been proposed: that they came from Asia, or that they were native to America.
sentences.translation: 人们提出了两个截然相反的起源说法：它们来自亚洲，或者它们原产于美洲。

structure.text: or that they were native to America

difficult_points: diametrically opposed 表示两个观点完全相反。
have been proposed 是现在完成时被动语态，表示这些起源说法已被提出。
冒号后两个 that 从句解释 Two diametrically opposed origins 的具体内容。
be native to 表示原产于某地。

sentences.sentence: Both suggestions have problems.
sentences.translation: 这两种说法都存在问题。
difficult_points: Both suggestions 指上一句中的两种起源假说。
have problems 表示这些假说各自有难以解释的地方。

sentences.sentence: In Asia, there is a large degree of coconut diversity and evidence of millennia of human use—but there are no relatives growing in the wild.
sentences.translation: 在亚洲，椰子的多样性很高，也有数千年人类使用椰子的证据——但那里没有野生的近缘植物。

structure.text: there is a large degree of coconut diversity and evidence of millennia of human use
a large degree of coconut diversity and evidence of millennia of human use
but there are no relatives growing in the wild

difficult_points: there is 引出两个并列证据：coconut diversity 和 evidence of human use。
millennia of human use 表示人类使用椰子的历史已有数千年。
破折号后的 but 引出反面证据：没有野生近缘植物。
growing in the wild 后置修饰 relatives。

sentences.sentence: In America, there are close coconut relatives, but no evidence that coconuts are indigenous.
sentences.translation: 在美洲，有椰子的近缘植物，却没有证据表明椰子是当地原生的。

structure.text: but no evidence that coconuts are indigenous

difficult_points: close coconut relatives 指与椰子亲缘关系近的植物，而不是椰子的亲属。
but no evidence 省略了 there is，完整含义是“但没有证据”。
that coconuts are indigenous 解释 evidence 的内容，说明缺少的是原生性的证据。

sentences.sentence: These problems have led to the intriguing suggestion that coconuts originated on coral islands in the Pacific and were dispersed from there.
sentences.translation: 这些问题催生了一个有趣的推测：椰子起源于太平洋的珊瑚岛，并从那里扩散开来。

structure.text: have led to the intriguing suggestion
that coconuts originated on coral islands in the Pacific and were dispersed from there
originated on coral islands in the Pacific and were dispersed from there

difficult_points: These problems 回指前两句中亚洲和美洲两种起源说各自的矛盾。
have led to 表示这些问题促成了新的解释或推测。
that 引导同位语从句，具体说明 suggestion 的内容。
were dispersed from there 是被动语态，there 指太平洋珊瑚岛。
